era: 2003-present. Hip-Hop. E=5 -- Ethiopian-American diaspora identity, Rainier Valley roots, politically engaged solo artistry. Fatbeats / independent. RETURN from degree 102 (46-degree gap). Solo work, Ethiopian diaspora, pan-African vision, anti-imperialist critique. Fifteenth degree in hip-hop era (134-148).

location.formative: Ethiopian-American. Grew up in Seattle's Rainier Valley. Part of one of the largest Ethiopian diaspora communities in the US. The Valley's Ethiopian geography -- restaurants, community organizations, churches -- is the landscape of his art.
location.career: Gabriel Teodros. Ethiopian-American MC. Solo albums, EPs, collaborations, spoken word. Fatbeats distribution. Pan-African vision. Anti-imperialist politics.

members.name: Gabriel Teodros
members.note: Ethiopian-American. Rainier Valley. Diaspora consciousness. Pan-African vision. Anti-imperialist critique. Poetic, dense lyricism.

associated_musicians.name: Common Market
associated_musicians.note: RA Scion + Sabzi. Rainier Valley introduced at 145. Teodros deepens at 148.

associated_musicians.name: Shabazz Palaces
associated_musicians.note: Ishmael Butler. Same Seattle hip-hop network. Afrofuturist connections.

associated_musicians.name: Ethiopian music community
associated_musicians.note: Ethiopian musicians, Amharic elements, pan-African collaborations. Cultural fusion.
